title: What is Alzheimer's Disease?
content: Alzheimer's disease is a progressive brain disorder that slowly destroys memory and thinking skills and, eventually, the ability to carry out the simplest tasks. It is the most common cause of dementia in older adults.

title: Symptoms of Alzheimer's
content: The most common early symptom of Alzheimer's is difficulty remembering newly learned information. As the disease advances, symptoms can include disorientation, mood and behavior changes; deepening confusion about events, time and place; unfounded suspicions about family, friends and professional caregivers; more serious memory loss and behavior changes; and difficulty speaking, swallowing and walking.

title: Stages of Alzheimer's
content: Alzheimer's disease typically progresses slowly in three general stages: early, middle, and late. Since Alzheimer's affects people in different ways, the timing and severity of dementia symptoms vary as each person progresses through the stages of Alzheimer's differently.

title: Risk Factors
content: The greatest known risk factor for Alzheimer's is increasing age, but the disease is not a normal part of aging. Family history is another strong risk factor. Other factors that may play a role include genetics, education, diet, and environment.

title: Treatment and Prevention
content: While there is no cure for Alzheimer's disease, there are treatments that can help slow the worsening of dementia symptoms and improve quality of life for those with Alzheimer's and their caregivers. There is no proven way to prevent Alzheimer's, but evidence suggests that a healthy lifestyle, including a healthy diet, physical activity, and cognitive stimulation, may help reduce the risk of cognitive decline.
